front: Work: narrated from the grave by a 'small winner' who recounts his death from pneumonia; dedicated to 'the worm who first gnawed' on his corpse

front: Work: Bento Santiago suspects wife Capitu of an affair with best friend Escobar in this Othello-infused novel
Work: after Escobar drowns, the narrator obsesses over his son Ezekiel's resemblance to the dead man
Work: Dona Gloria promises God her child will become a priest if it survives childbirth; Bento befriends Escobar at the seminary
Work: Rubiao inherits a fortune from the Humanitist philosopher and must care for an identically-named dog; 'to the victor, the potatoes!'
Work: Rubiao pursues the married Sophia and becomes deluded into thinking he is Napoleon III
Work: Dr. Bacamarte commits nearly everyone to an asylum called the Green House; locks himself in to cure himself of being sane
Work: in this novella, the doctor uses the motto 'plus ultra!' and argues almost everyone is insane using Socrates's daemon and Pascal's fear of the abyss
Author: this Brazilian author was the mestizo grandson of slaves; Harold Bloom called him the 'supreme black literary artist'
Author: this author's story 'Midnight Mass' opens 'I have never quite understood a conversation that I had with a lady many years ago, when I was seventeen and she was thirty'
Author: this author wrote Esau and Jacob, structured as Counselor Ayres's memoirs, about Natividade's children feuding for Flora's affections
Author: this author's story 'Life!' is a dialogue between Prometheus and the Wandering Jew
Author: in a story by this author, the philosophy of Pomadism (reality inferior to opinion) is preached by a bonze from the Kingdom of Bungo
